summary: The layers are arranged starting at the outermost surface inwardly as corneocytes followed closely by langerhans cells within the basal cell region; then deeper still lie melanocytes also found primarily near or just below these regions where merkle cells reside but don't form a distinct separate visible layer like others mentioned directly above/below one another structurally speaking
